description: Predefined Python error dataset for fast local detection — no AI call needed for known errors

ModuleNotFoundError.root_cause_template: The module '{module}' is not installed or not on the Python path. Run: pip install {module}
ModuleNotFoundError.simple: A required library is missing. You need to install it first.
ModuleNotFoundError.detailed: Python cannot find the specified module. It's either not installed (use pip install), not in the PYTHONPATH, or the import name is misspelled.

common_modules.STT: SpeechRecognition or a custom module — check if it exists in your project
common_modules.testttss: A custom TTS module — check if this file exists in your project directory

ImportError.root_cause_template: Cannot import '{name}' from '{module}'. The name may not exist in that module, or the module version is incompatible.
ImportError.simple: Something you're trying to import doesn't exist or isn't available.
ImportError.detailed: The import failed — either the name doesn't exist in the target module, the module has a version mismatch, or a DLL/shared library dependency is missing.

SyntaxError.root_cause_template: Python could not parse the code at line {line}. A character, keyword, or structure is invalid.
SyntaxError.simple: Your code has a typo or punctuation mistake that Python can't understand.
SyntaxError.detailed: Python's parser encountered a token it didn't expect. Common causes: missing colon after def/if/for/while, mismatched brackets, invalid indentation, or using Python 2 syntax in Python 3.

IndentationError.root_cause_template: Indentation is inconsistent or wrong at line {line}. Python uses indentation to define code blocks.
IndentationError.simple: Your code's spacing/indentation is wrong. Make sure you use consistent spaces or tabs.
IndentationError.detailed: Python uses indentation (spaces or tabs) to define code blocks. Mixing tabs and spaces, or using wrong indentation levels, causes this error.

patterns: inconsistent use of tabs and spaces
TabError.root_cause_template: The file mixes tabs and spaces for indentation.
TabError.simple: Your code mixes tabs and spaces. Pick one and stick to it.
TabError.detailed: Python 3 disallows mixing tabs and spaces in indentation. Use 'expand tabs' in your editor to convert all tabs to spaces.

patterns: argument of type .* is not iterable
TypeError.root_cause_template: A function or operation received a value of the wrong type.
TypeError.simple: You passed the wrong type of value to a function or operation.
TypeError.detailed: Python received a value of an incompatible type. Common causes: calling a non-callable, wrong number of arguments, operating on None, or subscripting a non-subscriptable object.

ValueError.root_cause_template: A function received an argument of the right type but an invalid value.
ValueError.simple: A value you provided is the right type but makes no sense for that operation.
ValueError.detailed: The value passed to a function is technically the right type but logically invalid — e.g., converting non-numeric string to int, sqrt of negative, or mismatched unpacking.

AttributeError.root_cause_template: Accessing attribute '{attr}' on an object that doesn't have it — often because the object is None.
AttributeError.simple: You tried to access something that doesn't exist on that object. It might be None.
AttributeError.detailed: The object doesn't have the attribute you're accessing. Frequent cause: a function returned None and you accessed a property on it without checking.

NameError.root_cause_template: Variable '{name}' is used before being defined, or is out of scope.
NameError.simple: You used a variable that hasn't been created yet.
NameError.detailed: Python can't find a variable with that name in the current scope. It was either never defined, defined inside a different scope, or has a typo.

KeyError.root_cause_template: Key '{key}' does not exist in the dictionary.
KeyError.simple: You tried to access a dictionary key that doesn't exist.
KeyError.detailed: The key you're accessing is not in the dictionary. Use .get(key, default) to avoid this, or check with 'key in dict' before accessing.

IndexError.root_cause_template: Accessing index {index} on a sequence that only has {length} elements.
IndexError.simple: You tried to access an item at a position that doesn't exist in the list.
IndexError.detailed: The index is beyond the bounds of the list/tuple/string. Check the length before accessing, or use negative indices correctly.

ZeroDivisionError.root_cause_template: Division or modulo operation where the divisor is zero.
ZeroDivisionError.simple: You tried to divide a number by zero, which is mathematically undefined.
ZeroDivisionError.detailed: The divisor evaluates to zero at runtime. Add a guard: 'if b != 0' before dividing, or use a try/except ZeroDivisionError block.

FileNotFoundError.root_cause_template: The file or directory '{path}' does not exist at the specified path.
FileNotFoundError.simple: The file you're trying to open doesn't exist.
FileNotFoundError.detailed: Python looked for the file at the specified path and it wasn't there. Check the path is correct relative to your working directory, or use os.path.exists() to check first.

PermissionError.simple: Your program doesn't have permission to access that file or directory.
PermissionError.detailed: The OS denied access to the file/directory. Run with elevated permissions, fix file ownership, or choose a different path.

OSError.root_cause_template: Operating system reported an error during I/O or system operation.
OSError.simple: Your program had trouble interacting with the operating system.
OSError.detailed: An OS-level error occurred — file I/O, network, or system call failure. Check the errno code for specifics.

RecursionError.root_cause_template: A function is calling itself indefinitely without a proper base case.
RecursionError.simple: A function kept calling itself too many times and Python stopped it.
RecursionError.detailed: Python hit its recursion limit (default 1000). Add a proper base case to your recursive function, or use sys.setrecursionlimit() if you genuinely need deep recursion.

MemoryError.root_cause_template: The program tried to allocate more memory than the system can provide.
MemoryError.simple: Your program ran out of memory.
MemoryError.detailed: Python tried to allocate too much memory. Common causes: loading a huge file at once, infinite list growth, or inefficient data structures.

TimeoutError.root_cause_template: An operation exceeded its time limit.
TimeoutError.simple: Something took too long and the program gave up waiting.
TimeoutError.detailed: A network call, subprocess, or blocking operation exceeded its timeout. Increase the timeout, check network connectivity, or optimize the slow operation.

ConnectionError.root_cause_template: Could not establish or maintain a network connection to the target.
ConnectionError.simple: Your program couldn't connect to the server.
ConnectionError.detailed: A network connection failed. The server may be down, the address is wrong, a firewall is blocking the connection, or the server closed it unexpectedly.

JSONDecodeError.root_cause_template: The string passed to json.loads() is not valid JSON.
JSONDecodeError.simple: The text you tried to parse as JSON is not valid JSON.
JSONDecodeError.detailed: json.loads() received a malformed JSON string. Check the source: it may be an error response, truncated data, or a string with single quotes instead of double.

UnicodeDecodeError.root_cause_template: Cannot decode bytes using '{encoding}' — the file may have a different encoding.
UnicodeDecodeError.simple: The file contains characters your program can't read with the current encoding.
UnicodeDecodeError.detailed: Python tried to decode bytes using the wrong encoding (usually UTF-8 vs latin-1). Open the file with encoding='utf-8', encoding='latin-1', or errors='ignore'.

UnicodeEncodeError.root_cause_template: Cannot encode the string to '{encoding}' — it contains characters outside that encoding's range.
UnicodeEncodeError.simple: Your string has special characters that can't be saved with the current encoding.
UnicodeEncodeError.detailed: The string contains characters that can't be represented in the target encoding. Use encode('utf-8', errors='replace') or ensure your output stream supports Unicode.

StopIteration.root_cause_template: Called next() on an exhausted iterator or generator.
StopIteration.simple: You tried to get the next item from something that has no more items.
StopIteration.detailed: next() was called on an exhausted iterator. Use a for loop instead, or wrap in try/except StopIteration.

AssertionError.root_cause_template: An assert statement evaluated to False.
AssertionError.simple: A safety check in the code failed.
AssertionError.detailed: An assert condition returned False. This usually indicates a contract violation — the program's state didn't match what the developer expected.

NotImplementedError.root_cause_template: A method was called that is declared but not yet implemented.
NotImplementedError.simple: You called a method that hasn't been written yet.
NotImplementedError.detailed: The method raises NotImplementedError intentionally, indicating it's an abstract method that subclasses must implement.

RuntimeError.root_cause_template: A generic runtime error occurred — see the message for specifics.
RuntimeError.simple: Something went wrong while your program was running.
RuntimeError.detailed: A general runtime condition failed. Common causes: modifying a dict/set while iterating it, thread limit reached, or re-entrant code.

OverflowError.root_cause_template: A numeric value exceeded the maximum representable size.
OverflowError.simple: A number got too big for Python to handle.
OverflowError.detailed: A float or integer exceeded representable limits. For floats this happens with exp() or similar — use decimal.Decimal for arbitrary precision.

SystemExit.root_cause_template: sys.exit() was called, terminating the program.
SystemExit.detailed: sys.exit() was invoked. If unexpected, search for sys.exit() calls or exit() calls in the code. Catch with except SystemExit if you need to clean up.

KeyboardInterrupt.root_cause_template: User pressed Ctrl+C to interrupt the running program.
KeyboardInterrupt.simple: You (or something) pressed Ctrl+C to stop the program.
KeyboardInterrupt.detailed: The user interrupted the program via keyboard signal. Handle with try/except KeyboardInterrupt if you need graceful shutdown.

EOFError.root_cause_template: input() or read() hit end-of-file with no data to return.
EOFError.simple: The program expected more input but there was none left.
EOFError.detailed: input() reached EOF (end of input stream). This happens when running scripts with piped stdin or in non-interactive environments.

IsADirectoryError.root_cause_template: Tried to open a directory path as if it were a file.
IsADirectoryError.simple: You tried to open a folder as a file.
IsADirectoryError.detailed: The path points to a directory, not a file. Use os.listdir() or pathlib to work with directories.

NotADirectoryError.root_cause_template: A path component in the given path is a file, not a directory.
NotADirectoryError.simple: Part of the path you're using is a file, not a folder.
NotADirectoryError.detailed: os.listdir() or os.chdir() was called on a path where one component is actually a file.

BlockingIOError.root_cause_template: A non-blocking I/O operation would have blocked.
BlockingIOError.simple: A network or file operation couldn't complete immediately.
BlockingIOError.detailed: A non-blocking socket or file operation returned EAGAIN/EWOULDBLOCK. Use asyncio or retry the operation.

ChildProcessError.root_cause_template: A child process operation failed — no child process exists.
ChildProcessError.simple: An operation on a subprocess failed.
ChildProcessError.detailed: os.wait() or related functions found no child process to wait for. Check that the subprocess was actually started.

patterns: will be removed in a future version
DeprecationWarning.root_cause_template: Using a deprecated API that will be removed in a future Python version.
DeprecationWarning.simple: You're using an old feature that Python plans to remove.
DeprecationWarning.detailed: The called API is deprecated. Update to the recommended replacement shown in the warning message.

FutureWarning.root_cause_template: Behavior of this operation will change in a future version.
FutureWarning.simple: This code might work differently in future Python versions.
FutureWarning.detailed: A FutureWarning signals that current behavior will change in a future release. Update your code now to avoid surprises later.

ResourceWarning.root_cause_template: A resource (file, socket) was not properly closed before being garbage collected.
ResourceWarning.simple: You forgot to close a file or network connection.
ResourceWarning.detailed: Python detected an unclosed resource. Use 'with' statements (context managers) to ensure files and sockets are always closed.

pickle.UnpicklingError.root_cause_template: The pickle file is corrupt, was saved with an incompatible Python version, or is not a pickle file at all.
pickle.UnpicklingError.simple: The file you're trying to load with pickle is broken or incompatible.
pickle.UnpicklingError.detailed: pickle.load() encountered an invalid or unreadable byte sequence. Never unpickle data from untrusted sources — it can execute arbitrary code.

ssl.SSLError.root_cause_template: SSL/TLS handshake or certificate verification failed.
ssl.SSLError.simple: A secure connection failed — usually a certificate problem.
ssl.SSLError.detailed: The SSL certificate is invalid, expired, self-signed, or the hostname doesn't match. You can disable verification with ssl=False (not recommended for production) or install certifi.

subprocess.CalledProcessError.root_cause_template: A subprocess exited with a non-zero return code.
subprocess.CalledProcessError.simple: A command your program ran failed.
subprocess.CalledProcessError.detailed: subprocess.check_call() or check_output() raised because the child process returned a failure exit code. Inspect CalledProcessError.stderr for details.

patterns: Failed to establish a new connection
requests.exceptions.ConnectionError.root_cause_template: Cannot connect to '{url}' — server unreachable, DNS failed, or network is down.
requests.exceptions.ConnectionError.simple: Your program can't reach the server. Check your internet or the server URL.
requests.exceptions.ConnectionError.detailed: The requests library failed to connect. DNS resolution failed or the host is unreachable. Verify the URL, check firewall rules, and test network connectivity.

requests.exceptions.HTTPError.simple: The server returned an error response.
requests.exceptions.HTTPError.detailed: The HTTP response had a 4xx (client error) or 5xx (server error) status code. Check the status code and response body for details.

custom_module_error.root_cause_template: Custom local module '{module}' not found. It must exist as a .py file in the same directory or be added to sys.path.
custom_module_error.simple: A local Python file that this code depends on is missing from the project folder.
custom_module_error.detailed: This module is a custom local file, not a pip package. Make sure '{module}.py' exists in the same directory as the file trying to import it.
custom_module_error.fix_template: Ensure '{module}.py' exists in the project directory, or add its path: import sys; sys.path.insert(0, '/path/to/module')
